results.seo_title: Improving AI Model Explainability for Safer Predictions
results.meta_description: A new approach helps users trust AI model predictions in safety-critical applications like healthcare and autonomous driving.
results.body_html:
A new approach could help users know whether to trust a model’s predictions in safety-critical applications like health care and autonomous driving.

Key Benefits of Explainable AI

  • Improved accuracy in safety-critical applications
  • Clearer, more concise explanations for users
  • Reduced reliance on previously defined concepts

How it Works

Step Description
1 The model extracts knowledge from the target model and translates it into plain-language concepts.
2 The specialized machine-learning models force the model to use these concepts, producing better explanations.

Potential Applications

Any pretrained computer vision model can be converted into one that uses concepts to explain its reasoning.

This approach has the potential to improve the accuracy and trustworthiness of AI models in various industries, including healthcare and autonomous driving.

source.title: Improving AI models’ ability to explain their predictions
source.summary: A new approach could help users know whether to trust a model’s predictions in safety-critical applications like health care and autonomous driving.

Participating Organizations

Evaluation Metrics

Key metrics for evaluating AGI will include measures of cognitive ability, such as reasoning and problem-solving.

source.title: Measuring progress toward AGI: A cognitive framework
source.summary: We’re introducing a framework to measure progress toward AGI, and launching a Kaggle hackathon to build the relevant evaluations.

results.seo_title: How Long of Self-Supervised Learning Affects DR Screening Accuracy
results.meta_description: Understanding the impact of self-supervised learning on diabetic retinopathy screening accuracy and reliability.
results.body_html:
Self-supervised learning can improve DR screening accuracy, but only up to a point.

SSL pretraining improves selective prediction compared to training from scratch. Larger pretraining duration does not consistently improve reliability. Calibrated confidence and confidence-based abstention are crucial for safe DR screening.

Diabetic Retinopathy Grading Dataset Fixed fine-tuning protocol with multiple SSL checkpoints.

Results and Discussion Our results show that while longer pretraining duration may improve accuracy, it does not consistently improve reliability. This highlights the importance of abstention-aware evaluation in safe DR screening.

Conclusion The findings of this study underscore the need for careful consideration of self-supervised learning duration in DR screening tasks. By prioritizing calibrated confidence and confidence-based abstention, we can improve the accuracy and reliability of these models.

source.title: Knowing When Not to Predict: Self Supervised Learning and Abstention for Safer DR Screening
source.summary: Researchers have found that self-supervised learning (SSL) for medical image models, such as those used in diabetic retinopathy screening, can improve selective prediction but not necessarily increase reliability. The length of SSL pretraining has a significant impact on the model's confidence and ability to make accurate predictions, with longer pretraining not always leading to improved results. This highlights the need for abstention-aware evaluation and suggests that pretraining length should be considered an important design choice in safety-critical applications.

results.seo_title: How PneumoNet Enables Adaptive AI for Point-of-Care Pneumonia…
results.meta_description: PneumoNet achieves 86.6% accuracy in point-of-care pneumonia diagnosis with adaptive, privacy-preserving AI on resource-limited devices.
results.body_html:
PneumoNet achieves 86.6% accuracy in point-of-care pneumonia diagnosis with adaptive, privacy-preserving AI on resource-limited devices.

A lightweight CNN for on-device prediction A dual-stage balanced buffer for class-balanced replay A dynamic class-weighted loss to correct training-batch imbalances

PneumoNet's Performance and Advantages

PneumoNet's Potential for Real-World Healthcare Enabling adaptive, privacy-preserving diagnostic AI directly on point-of-care medical devices in real-world and pandemic-ready healthcare.

source.title: On-Device Continual Learning with Dual-Stage Buffer and Dynamic Loss for Point-of-Care Pneumonia Diagnosis
source.summary: Researchers have developed a deep learning method called PneumoNet for detecting pneumonia from chest X-rays in resource-limited settings. The model uses a lightweight CNN and a dual-stage buffer to handle domain shifts, achieving high accuracy of 86.6% with minimal forgetting. This approach has the potential to enable adaptive and privacy-preserving diagnostic AI directly on point-of-care medical devices. PneumoNet is smaller and faster than existing baselines, making it suitable for real-world healthcare applications.

results.seo_title: Evaluating the Ethics of Autonomous Systems: A New Framework for…
results.meta_description: MIT researchers develop an automated evaluation method to balance measurable outcomes and qualitative values in autonomous systems, ensuring fairness and…
results.body_html:
Are AI-driven autonomous systems fair? MIT researchers have developed a testing framework to pinpoint situations where AI decision-support systems are not treating people and communities fairly.

Identifying Fairness in Autonomous Systems

  • A low-cost power distribution strategy can leave disadvantaged neighborhoods more vulnerable to outages than higher-income areas.
  • The system separates objective evaluations from user-defined human values, using a large language model as a proxy for humans to capture and incorporate stakeholder preferences.

Automated Evaluation Method

The adaptive framework selects the best scenarios for further evaluation, streamlining a process that typically requires costly and time-consuming manual effort.

Key Features
Measurable outcomes (cost or reliability)Qualitative or subjective values (fairness)

Predicting Ethical Dilemmas

“We can insert a lot of rules and guardrails into AI systems, but those safeguards can only prevent the things we can imagine happening. It is not enough to say, ‘Let’s just use AI because it has been trained on this information.’ We wanted to develop a more systematic way to discover the unknown unknowns and have a way to predict them before anything bad happens,”
— senior author Chuchu Fan, MIT Department of Aeronautics and Astronautics.

Read time: 8 minutes

source.title: Evaluating the ethics of autonomous systems
source.summary: MIT researchers developed a testing framework that pinpoints situations where AI decision-support systems are not treating people and communities fairly.

results.seo_title: What Makes Work Valuable According to Michal Masny
results.meta_description: Michal Masny, NC Ethics of Technology Postdoctoral Fellow, discusses the value of work and its impact on well-being.
results.body_html:
Work is both necessary and positively valuable, according to Michal Masny. He argues that our lives might be worsened if we were to eliminate work completely, but there can be optimal combinations of work and leisure time.

Develop excellence at something Make a social contribution Gain social recognition Create and sustain community

Challenges to Traditional Education

The traditional division of labor is that scientists and engineers invent new technologies, and then philosophers and lawyers evaluate and regulate them. GroupRole Scientists and EngineersInvent new technologies Philosophers and LawyersEvaluate and regulate technologies

Redefining the Role of Philosophers in Technology “I want to create scientists who think about their projects and potential outcomes as lawyers and philosophers might, and vice versa,” says Masny.

The Importance of Eliminating the ‘Wisdom Gap’

Citing scientist Carl Sagan’s warning about the dangers of becoming “powerful without becoming commensurately wise” The pace at which new technologies are invented and deployed has made this division of labor unworkable.

source.title: A philosophy of work
source.summary: As the NC Ethics of Technology Postdoctoral Fellow, Michal Masny is advancing dialogue, teaching, and research into the social and ethical dimensions of new computing technologies.

results.seo_title: MIT Researchers Enable Privacy-Preserving AI Training on Everyday…
results.meta_description: A new method developed by MIT researchers can accelerate privacy-preserving AI training by 81%, enabling more accurate models on resource-constrained edge…
results.body_html:
A new method developed by MIT researchers can accelerate privacy-preserving AI training by 81%, enabling more accurate models on resource-constrained edge devices.

Accelerating Federated LearningThe MIT researchers boosted the efficiency of a technique known as federated learning, which involves a network of connected devices that work together to train a shared AI model.Data are kept secure because they remain on each device.This approach could make it more feasible for AI models to be used in high-stakes applications with strict security and privacy standards, like health care and finance.Overcoming Memory ConstraintsDevice LimitationsSolutionVarying memory capacity and computational capabilityThe new approach is designed to handle a heterogenous network of wireless devices with varied limitations.Communication bottlenecksThe

method overcomes these bottlenecks, enabling faster training performance.Enabling AI on Small Devices“This work is about bringing AI to small devices where it is not currently possible to run these kinds of powerful models.”

source.title: Enabling privacy-preserving AI training on everyday devices
source.summary: A new method could bring more accurate and efficient AI models to high-stakes applications like health care and finance, even in under-resourced settings.

results.seo_title: How to Debias AI Vision Models: A Smarter Approach
results.meta_description: A new debiasing technique called WRING avoids creating or amplifying biases in AI vision models, improving accuracy and safety in medical applications.
results.body_html:
A new debiasing technique called WRING avoids creating or amplifying biases that can occur with existing debiasing approaches. A new paper from researchers at MIT, Worcester Polytechnic Institute, and Google proposes a novel debiasing approach called “Weighted Rotational DebiasING” (i.e., WRING) that can be applied to vision language models (VLMs), like OpenAI’s OpenCLIP.

Understanding the Problem of Bias in AI Vision Models

  • Bias in AI vision models can lead to inaccurate predictions and poor performance in real-world settings.
  • In high-stakes medical scenarios, bias can have serious consequences.

The Limitations of Projection Debiasing

“When you do that, you inadvertently squish everything around,” says Walter Gerych, the paper’s first author. “All the other relationships that the model learns change when you do that.”

A Smarter Approach: WRING

Key Features of WRING
Removes undesirable, biased information from model embeddingsReduces the risk of amplifying biases in AI vision models

Benefits of WRING

WRING offers a more effective and efficient approach to debiasing AI vision models, improving accuracy and safety in medical applications.

source.title: Solving the “Whac-a-mole dilemma”: A smarter way to debias AI vision models
source.summary: A new debiasing technique called WRING avoids creating or amplifying biases that can occur with existing debiasing approaches.

results.seo_title: Apple's Siri Revamp Could Include Auto-Deleting Chats: What You Need…
results.meta_description: Apple is revamping Siri with a focus on privacy, but what does this mean for users and the tech industry? Learn more about the implications of auto-deleting…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Apple's new Siri revamp may include auto-deleting chats, a move aimed at prioritizing user privacy.

Enhanced privacy controls: The revamped Siri will offer more granular control over user data, allowing users to delete their chat history with greater ease. Improved security measures: Apple is reportedly incorporating advanced security features to protect user data from potential threats. More natural language processing capabilities: The updated Siri will be able to understand and respond to more complex queries, making it a more effective virtual assistant.

User data protection becomes a priority: As Apple prioritizes user privacy, other tech companies may be forced to follow suit. The industry will need to adapt to new regulations and standards for data protection, potentially leading to increased costs and complexity.

Increased demand for secure virtual assistants: With the emphasis on user privacy, there may be a growing demand for more secure virtual assistants that can protect user data. The market for secure virtual assistants is likely to expand, with companies looking to capitalize on this trend.

Timeline and Availability Apple has not yet announced a specific release date for the revamped Siri, but it is expected to roll out in the coming months.

source.title: Apple’s Siri revamp could include auto-deleting chats
source.summary: Privacy will be a major theme when Apple unveils a new version of Siri.

results.seo_title: SwordBench Evaluates Orthogonality of Steering Image Representations
results.meta_description: A new benchmark for steering image representations of vision models, SwordBench evaluates the second-order effects of orthogonalization among concept…
results.body_html:
SwordBench evaluates the orthogonality of steering image representations in vision models. This benchmark assesses the second-order effects of orthogonalization among concept activation vectors for pragmatic steering.

Key Evaluation Notions

  • Cross-concept robustness measures the stability of concept detection performance across inputs orthogonalized against alternative concepts.
  • Collateral damage quantifies whether steering inadvertently affects model performance on a downstream task for inputs lacking the bias.

Comparison with Baselines

ModelSeparability and OrthogonalityCollateral Damage
Linear Support Vector MachineSuperior separability and orthogonalityFails to achieve zero collateral damage
Sparse AutoencodersTrailing sparse autoencodersN/A

Availability of Source Code

The source code for SwordBench will be made available soon on GitHub.

source.title: SwordBench: Evaluating Orthogonality of Steering Image Representations
source.summary: Steering or intervening on model representations at inference time to correct predictions is essential for AI interpretability and safety, yet existing evaluation protocols are limited to ambiguous language modeling tasks. To address this gap, we introduce SwordBench, a benchmark for steering image representations of vision models across multiple backbones and concept removal tasks. Beyond a unified benchmarking suite, we propose new evaluation notions that uncover the second-order effects of orthogonalization among concept activation vectors for pragmatic steering. Specifically, cross-concept robustness measures the stability of concept detection performance across inputs orthogonalized against alternative concepts, and collateral damage quantifies whether steering inadvertently affects model performance on a downstream task for inputs lacking the bias. We find that although a linear support vector machine exhibits superior separability and orthogonality, it fails to achieve zero collateral damage, often trailing sparse autoencoders. In simpler regimes, both standard baselines and optimization-based methods fail to achieve perfect steering. The source code will be made available soon on GitHub.

results.seo_title: Cross-Source Supervision for Bone Infection Segmentation in…
results.meta_description: A new study proposes a novel approach to improve the accuracy of bone infection segmentation using dual-modality PET-CT fusion, providing a robust paradigm…
results.body_html:
Early and accurate diagnosis of bone infections is crucial for clinical treatment, as PET-CT integrates anatomical information from CT with metabolic information from PET.

Challenges in Bone Infection Segmentation

Indistinct lesion boundaries and inconsistencies in annotations generated by different experts or automated systems Traditional two-dimensional evaluation methods are often used, which can lead to performance inflation caused by inter-slice correlation in small datasets

Novel Approach to Bone Infection Segmentation

bimodal end-to-end segmentation framework Integrates PET metabolic signals and CT bone-window anatomy through an early-fusion multimodal representation

decoupled dual-source learning framework Trains parallel models on independent expert annotations driven by high-sensitivity and high-specificity clinical intents

Experimental Results and Evaluation Experimental results objectively report performance variations at the patient level (Mean + SD and Mean - SD), demonstrating the effectiveness of multimodal PET-CT fusion.

The proposed approach provides a robust, diversity-preserving paradigm for clinical AI deployment in bone infection segmentation Future work should focus on exploring the application of this approach to other medical imaging modalities

source.title: Cross-Source Supervision for Bone Infection Segmentation in Dual-Modality PET-CT
source.summary: Early and accurate diagnosis and lesion localization of bone infections are crucial for clinical treatment. PET-CT integrates anatomical information from CT with metabolic information from PET, making it an important imaging modality for diagnosing bone infections. However, accurate lesion segmentation remains challenging due to indistinct lesion boundaries and inconsistencies in annotations generated by different experts or automated systems. In this work, we investigate multimodal segmentation of bone infections under annotation discrepancy. We develop a bimodal end-to-end segmentation framework that integrates PET metabolic signals and CT bone-window anatomy through an early-fusion multimodal representation.To mitigate performance inflation caused by inter-slice correlation in small datasets, this study discards traditional two-dimensional evaluation methods and implements a rigorous patient-level 3D volumetric evaluation and cross-validation. Furthermore, instead of forcing a singular consensus, we propose a decoupled dual-source learning framework where parallel models are trained on independent expert annotations driven by high-sensitivity and high-specificity clinical intents. Experimental results objectively report performance variations at the patient level (Mean + SD and Mean - SD), demonstrating the effectiveness of multimodal PET-CT fusion. The cross-evaluation matrix quantitatively reveals how models successfully internalize distinct expert diagnostic philosophies, providing a robust, diversity-preserving paradigm for clinical AI deployment in bone infection segmentation.

results.seo_title: How Adam Fixes Stochastic Gradient Descent's Frequency Bias
results.meta_description: Stochastic gradient descent's frequency bias is a hidden optimization challenge. Learn how Adam fixes it and its impact on machine learning models.
results.body_html:
Stochastic gradient descent's frequency bias occurs when parameters associated with common tokens receive constant gradient updates, while parameters tied to rare tokens may go hundreds of iterations without updating.

Model performance suffers due to biased optimization Rare but meaningful tokens are often overlooked Convergence rate is lower than expected

Benchmarks and Gaps in Stochastic Gradient Descent

Parameter Expected Update Frequency Actual Update Frequency

Common tokens Constant updates Hundreds of iterations without update

Solution: Adam Optimization Algorithm Adam adjusts the learning rate for each parameter based on the magnitude of the gradient, which helps to mitigate the frequency bias.

Conclusion and Future Work The impact of stochastic gradient descent's frequency bias on machine learning models is significant. Further research is needed to develop more efficient optimization algorithms that can effectively address this challenge.

source.title: Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD’s) Frequency Bias and How Adam Fixes It
source.summary:
Modern language models are trained on data with extremely uneven token distributions. A small number of words appear in almost every sentence, while many rare but meaningful tokens occur only occasionally. This creates a hidden optimization challenge: parameters associated with common tokens receive constant gradient updates, while parameters tied to rare tokens may go hundreds […]

The post Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD’s) Frequency Bias and How Adam Fixes It  appeared first on MarkTechPost.
